name: Kazan (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the silver field of the city's medieval coat of arms — purity and peace
the lower stripe — fertility and the Tatar national colour
the gold of the Zilant's crown

description: A white field with a narrow green stripe along the bottom, charged centrally with the Zilant — a winged dragon-like creature derived from Tatar legend — under a gold crown.
history: The Zilant has been the emblem of Kazan since at least the 16th century, recorded on imperial Russian seals and grants. The modern city flag, restoring the Zilant on a white-and-green field, was adopted on 24 December 2004 by the Kazan City Duma.

features.role: Zilant — the winged dragon-like creature of Tatar legend
features.symbolism: the historic emblem of Kazan, recorded on imperial seals from the 16th century
